skill.name: business-consistency
skill.description: Maintain consistent business name, address, phone, profile, and schema data across citation platforms and local SEO workflows.
skill.summary: Keep business identity data consistent across directories, profiles, audits, and structured data.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, schema.org example URLs, and keyword matches as security concerns. Manual review found documentation content only, with no executable scripts, network requests, shell commands, credential access, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks around table names, JSON examples, and status diagrams. These are documentation markers, not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged tokens are visible Markdown formatting for documentation and examples. No executable code block instructs command execution.

low_findings.title: Schema.org URLs Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs appear only as JSON-LD context values for schema markup examples. The skill does not make HTTP requests or send data externally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs are standard schema.org context strings inside example JSON. There is no fetch, curl, browser automation, or network-capable tool instruction.

low_findings.title: Keyword-Based Blocker Alerts Are Benign Text
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography and network reconnaissance alerts appear to be substring matches in prose, business data labels, platform names, and industry categories. No cryptographic algorithm use or reconnaissance command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found descriptive business and platform text at these lines. No code path performs encryption, hashing, scanning, probing, or external enumeration.

content.value_statement: Business details often drift across directories, profiles, and schema markup. This skill gives teams a clear source-of-truth workflow for audits, updates, and local SEO consistency.
actual_capabilities: Defines a source-of-truth model for business name, address, phone, and profile data.
Organizes platform priorities across search, maps, social, directory, and industry listings.
Guides weekly and monthly audit workflows with scoring and issue reporting.
Outlines LocalBusiness and Organization schema markup fields for consistent structured data.
Tracks citation status from not claimed through verification, update needs, and suspension.
limitations: Does not connect directly to listing platforms or update profiles automatically.
Does not verify legal business registration, licenses, or tax identifiers.
Requires users to provide the current source-of-truth business data.
Focuses on Australian examples, so other regions may need platform changes.

use_cases.title: Clean up local SEO citations
use_cases.description: Compare current directory listings against a master business record and identify mismatched NAP fields.

use_cases.title: Prepare a location launch
use_cases.description: Create a consistent checklist for new location profiles, business hours, service areas, and schema fields.

use_cases.title: Review structured business data
use_cases.description: Check whether LocalBusiness or Organization schema reflects the same entity details used in listings.

prompt_templates.title: Create a master record
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a business consistency master record for this company. Include legal name, trading name, address, phone, website, email, hours, categories, service areas, and Australian identifiers.

prompt_templates.title: Audit priority platforms
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these platform listings against the master record. Score Tier 1 and Tier 2 consistency, list mismatches, and recommend exact field updates.

prompt_templates.title: Generate schema guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this master business data to prepare LocalBusiness and Organization schema guidance. Explain which fields must match listings exactly.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a multi-location rollout
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a consistency workflow for multiple business locations. Include source-of-truth ownership, audit cadence, citation status tracking, issue severity, and reporting metrics.

output_examples.input: A monthly audit found different phone formats on Google, Facebook, and Yellow Pages.
output_examples.output: The output identifies the approved phone format, lists each mismatched platform, assigns priority by tier, and recommends exact replacement values.

output_examples.input: A business is opening a new Brisbane location and needs listings prepared.
output_examples.output: The output provides a launch checklist covering master record fields, Tier 1 platforms, Australian directories, schema fields, and verification status.

output_examples.input: A website manager needs to align schema markup with citation data.
output_examples.output: The output maps business identity fields to schema concepts and highlights details that must match directory listings exactly.

best_practices: Approve one master business record before updating any listing or schema field.
Audit highest-impact platforms first, especially search, maps, and major social profiles.
Record every mismatch with platform name, current value, approved value, and update status.
anti_patterns: Updating directories from memory without checking the master record.
Using different phone, address, or category formats for different platforms.
Publishing schema markup that conflicts with Google Business Profile or directory data.

faq.question: What does NAP consistency mean?
faq.answer: NAP means name, address, and phone. These fields should match across listings, profiles, and structured data.

faq.question: Can this skill update Google Business Profile automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides audit and planning guidance, but users must update platforms through their own accounts.

faq.question: Does it support businesses outside Australia?
faq.answer: Yes, but the included platform and identifier examples are Australia-focused and may need regional changes.

faq.question: What data should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the approved legal name, trading name, full address, phone, website, email, hours, categories, and service areas.

faq.question: How often should listings be audited?
faq.answer: Use weekly spot checks for critical platforms and monthly full audits for priority directories and profiles.

faq.question: Can it help with schema markup?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines LocalBusiness and Organization schema fields that should align with the master business record.
